_note: Slab c.100-0 BC — the FALL OF THE ROMAN REPUBLIC and the RISE OF AUGUSTUS. All figures HISTORICAL. House = ANTIQUITY (JUL_ prefix routes there, joining the existing Julii & Julio-Claudians; ROM_ would wrongly route to the separate 'Alban Kings' page). Builds directly onto existing ANTIQUITY nodes and NEVER re-parents them: JUL_JULIACAESARIS (Caesar's daughter Julia), MARKANTONY, OCTAVIA, AUGUSTUS, DOMITIUSAHEN, NERODRUSUS and AGRIPPINAELDER are referenced by id only; where an existing person gains ancestry a NEW parent-union is added above them, and where an existing union gains a sibling the SAME two parent ids are re-stated so the build merely APPENDS (per model.py). CROSS-LINE MARRIAGES (priority): (1) POMPEY THE GREAT wed Caesar's daughter Julia — the union that sealed the First Triumvirate; childless (Julia died in childbirth 54 BC), so it ties the two houses without issue. (2) The assassin BRUTUS wed PORCIA, daughter of CATO THE YOUNGER — linking the tyrannicide to the Republic's last champion. (3) CASSIUS wed Junia Tertia, half-sister of Brutus (noted). (4) Antony's daughter ANTONIA MAJOR (by Octavia) married L. Domitius Ahenobarbus and bore the EXISTING Gnaeus Domitius Ahenobarbus (DOMITIUSAHEN, Nero's father) — tying Nero's paternal line back to Antony & Octavia. (5) Livia's sons by her FIRST husband were the emperor Tiberius & the EXISTING Drusus the Elder (NERODRUSUS); Livia then wed AUGUSTUS. (6) Augustus's only child JULIA THE ELDER (by Scribonia) married AGRIPPA and bore Gaius, Lucius, the EXISTING Agrippina the Elder (AGRIPPINAELDER, mother of Agrippina Younger -> Nero), Julia the Younger & Agrippa Postumus — anchoring the whole Julio-Claudian file to Augustus. Marcus Junius Brutus the assassin (85-42 BC) is DISTINCT from Lucius Junius Brutus, founder of the Republic (ROM_BRUTUS, in 26b); Cato the Younger is DISTINCT from Cato the Elder (in 29b) — both noted in labels. Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia 'Pompey', 'Wives of Pompey the Great', 'Mucia Tertia', 'Sextus Pompeius', 'Marcus Licinius Crassus', 'Cato the Younger', 'Porcia (wife of Brutus)', 'Marcus Junius Brutus', 'Gaius Cassius Longinus', 'Junia Tertia', 'Cicero', 'Fulvia', 'Marcus Antonius Antyllus', 'Iullus Antonius', 'Antonia Major', 'Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus (consul 16 BC)', 'Gnaeus Domitius Ahenobarbus (father of Nero)', 'Livia', 'Tiberius', 'Julia the Elder', 'Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa'. NO fabrication — attested lines followed; unrecorded points drawn as placeholders, not invented. Ordered so every referenced id exists before use.

families.note: FIRST TRIUMVIRATE — the alliance-sealing CROSS-LINE marriage: Pompey the Great ● the EXISTING Julia, Caesar's daughter. Childless (Julia died in childbirth 54 BC), her death helped unravel the triumvirate. JUL_JULIACAESARIS referenced by id, not re-created.

a.label:
Pompey the Great
106-48 BC · Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus, triumvir & conqueror of the East; rival of Caesar, beaten at Pharsalus & murdered in Egypt · historical

families.note: Pompey's surviving children were by his third wife, Mucia Tertia.

b.label:
Mucia Tertia
c.95 BC · third wife of Pompey; mother of his children · historical

children.label:
Gnaeus Pompeius the Younger
c.75-45 BC · elder son of Pompey; fought on after Pharsalus, killed following defeat at Munda · historical

Sextus Pompeius
67-35 BC · younger son of Pompey; led the naval war against the triumvirs from Sicily · historical

families.note: The third triumvir — Crassus, the richest man in Rome, destroyed by Parthia at Carrhae, 53 BC.

a.label:
Marcus Licinius Crassus
c.115-53 BC · richest man in Rome & triumvir; his army was annihilated by Parthia at Carrhae, 53 BC, where he died · historical

b.label:
Tertulla
c.100 BC · wife of Crassus · historical

children.label:
Publius Licinius Crassus
c.86-53 BC · son of Crassus; Caesar's cavalry officer in Gaul, fell beside his father at Carrhae · historical

families.note: THE ASSASSINS — Cato the Younger (distinct from Cato the Elder), Stoic champion of the Republic, and his daughter Porcia.

a.label:
Cato the Younger
95-46 BC · Marcus Porcius Cato Uticensis (NOT Cato the Elder); Stoic senator & incorruptible foe of Caesar; took his own life at Utica rather than live under a dictator · historical

b.label:
Atilia
c.85 BC · first wife of Cato the Younger; mother of Porcia · historical

children.label:
Porcia
c.70-43 BC · daughter of Cato the Younger; wife of the assassin Brutus & the only woman privy to the plot against Caesar · historical

families.note: CROSS-LINE marriage — the assassin Brutus ● Porcia, daughter of Cato the Younger, joining the tyrannicide to the Republic's last champion. Marcus Junius Brutus is DISTINCT from Lucius Junius Brutus the founder (ROM_BRUTUS). Childless.

a.label:
Marcus Junius Brutus
85-42 BC · foremost of Caesar's assassins on the Ides of March (NOT Lucius Junius Brutus, founder of the Republic); beaten at Philippi, took his own life · historical

families.note: The other lead assassin — Cassius ● Junia Tertia, half-sister of Brutus (a noted cross-tie). Childless in the tree.

a.label:
Gaius Cassius Longinus
c.85-42 BC · prime mover of Caesar's assassination; defeated at Philippi, took his own life · historical

b.label:
Junia Tertia
c.75 BC-22 AD · wife of Cassius; half-sister of the assassin Brutus · historical

families.note: Cicero the orator, defender of the Republic, and his family.

a.label:
Marcus Tullius Cicero
106-43 BC · greatest Roman orator, statesman & philosopher; defender of the Republic, proscribed & killed by the Second Triumvirate · historical

b.label:
Terentia
c.98 BC · wife of Cicero · historical

children.label:
Tullia
c.79-45 BC · beloved daughter of Cicero, whose death in childbirth broke him · historical

Marcus Tullius Cicero Minor
65 BC-? · son of Cicero; later consul under Augustus · historical

families.note: SECOND TRIUMVIRATE — Mark Antony's marriage to Fulvia (the EXISTING MARKANTONY referenced by id) and their sons.

b.label:
Fulvia
c.83-40 BC · fierce wife of Mark Antony; the first Roman woman depicted on coinage · historical

children.label:
Marcus Antonius Antyllus
47-30 BC · elder son of Antony & Fulvia; executed by Octavian after Actium · historical

Iullus Antonius
43-2 BC · son of Antony & Fulvia; consul 10 BC, later condemned over Julia the Elder · historical

families.note: Antony's OTHER marriage — to the EXISTING Octavia, sister of Augustus. This APPENDS Antonia Major to their existing union (05 already holds Antonia Minor; she is NOT re-added). Both parents referenced by id.

children.label:
Antonia Major
39 BC-c.29 AD · elder daughter of Antony & Octavia; paternal grandmother of the emperor Nero · historical

families.note: CROSS-LINE — Antonia Major ● Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus bore the EXISTING Gnaeus Domitius Ahenobarbus (DOMITIUSAHEN in 05, Nero's father). This ADDS ancestry above DOMITIUSAHEN (referenced by id, NOT re-parented), tying Nero's paternal line back to Antony & Octavia.

a.label:
Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus
c.49 BC · consul 16 BC; husband of Antonia Major · historical

families.note: AUGUSTUS'S CIRCLE — Livia's FIRST marriage, to Tiberius Claudius Nero, produced the emperor Tiberius and the EXISTING Drusus the Elder (NERODRUSUS in 05). This ADDS ancestry above NERODRUSUS (referenced by id, NOT re-parented) and introduces Livia before her marriage to Augustus.

a.label:
Tiberius Claudius Nero
c.85-33 BC · first husband of Livia; father of the emperor Tiberius & Drusus the Elder · historical

b.label:
Livia Drusilla
58 BC-29 AD · wife of Augustus, empress & matriarch of the Julio-Claudian dynasty · historical

children.label:
👑 Tiberius
42 BC-37 AD · second Roman Emperor; stepson & heir of Augustus · historical

families.note: The dynastic marriage — Augustus ● Livia (both referenced by id). They had no surviving children together; the union is the political heart of the new order.
Augustus's ONLY child, Julia the Elder, was by his second wife Scribonia. AUGUSTUS referenced by id.

b.label:
Scribonia
c.70 BC · second wife of Augustus; mother of his only child, Julia · historical

children.label:
Julia the Elder
39 BC-14 AD · only child of Augustus; wife of Agrippa & mother of his heirs; exiled by her father for adultery · historical

families.note: CROSS-LINE anchor — Agrippa ● Julia the Elder. Their children include the EXISTING Agrippina the Elder (AGRIPPINAELDER in 05, mother of Agrippina the Younger -> Nero). Referencing AGRIPPINAELDER by id ADDS her ancestry (NOT re-parented) and ties the entire Julio-Claudian file down to Augustus & Agrippa.

a.label:
Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa
63-12 BC · Augustus's great general & son-in-law; architect of the victory at Actium, 31 BC · historical

children.label:
Gaius Caesar
20 BC-4 AD · grandson & adopted heir of Augustus; died young of a wound in the East · historical

Lucius Caesar
17 BC-2 AD · grandson & adopted heir of Augustus; died young at Massilia · historical

Julia the Younger
19 BC-c.29 AD · granddaughter of Augustus; exiled for adultery like her mother · historical

Agrippa Postumus
12 BC-14 AD · posthumous son of Agrippa; adopted then disowned & exiled by Augustus, killed at his death · historical
